title: The Australian Stockman
content:
آ آ آ آ The sun peers oï؟½er you wooded ridge and throï؟½ the forest dense,
آ آ آ آ Its golden edge oï؟½er the mountain ledge looks down on the stockyard fence,
آ آ آ آ Looks down, looks down, looks down on the stockyard fence;
آ آ آ آ And dark creeks rush throï؟½ the tangled brush, when their shuddering shadows throng
آ آ آ آ Until they chime in the rude rough rhyme of the wild goburraï؟½s song.

آ آ آ آ آ آ آ آ آ آ آ آ آ آ آ آ آ آ آ آ آ آ آ آ آ آ آ آ آ آ آ آ آ آ آ آ آ آ آ آ  Chorus

آ آ آ آ آ آ آ آ  Till they chime, ha! ha! till they chime, ha! ha! in the wild goburraï؟½s song;
آ آ آ آ آ آ آ آ  Till they chime, ha! ha! till they chime, ha! ha! in the wild goburraï؟½s song.

آ آ آ آ The night owl to her home hath fled, to shun the glorious pomp
آ آ آ آ Of golden day she speeds away to her nest in the tea-tree swamp;
آ آ آ آ Away, away to her nest in the tea-tree swamp.

آ آ آ آ The dingo looks with a timid stare as he stealthily prowls along,
آ آ آ آ And his pattering feet in concert beat with the wild goburraï؟½s song.

آ آ آ آ آ آ آ آ  Chorus: And they beat, ha! ha! &c.

آ آ آ آ Oh! let them boast their cityï؟½s wealth, who toil in a dusty town;
آ آ آ آ Give me the beam on the mountain stream, and the rangeï؟½s dark-faced frownï؟½
آ آ آ آ The stream, the stream, and the rangeï؟½s dark-faced frown.
آ آ آ آ When our steed shall pass oï؟½er the quivï؟½ring grass, and the crack of the sounding thong
آ آ آ آ Shall bid the startled echoes join the wild goburraï؟½s song.

آ آ آ آ آ آ آ آ  Chorus: And they join, ha! ha! &c.
meaning:
"The Australian Stockman" is a quintessential example of Banjo Paterson (Andrew Barton)'s signature style...

### Why We Love This Line
At Linespedia, we believe that poetry is the ultimate sanctuary for the soul...
